diagnostic-response.title: Complete Diagnostic Findings for Remove Background Upload Failure and Build Issues

answer.question: Exact client-side code that constructs and sends the upload request

analysis.critical-issue: Line 376-379 sends POST to /api/images/direct-upload with NO BODY. The request contains only headers (x-api-key), but content-length would be 0. This empty request is proxied to the Cloudflare Worker, which may fail.

answer.question: Two-step Cloudflare direct upload or single-step proxy?

step.description: Client POSTs to /api/images/direct-upload (empty body, x-api-key header)
step.purpose: Obtain signed uploadURL from Cloudflare Images API

step.description: Client POSTs FormData directly to uploadURL from Step 1
step.purpose: Upload image blob directly to Cloudflare Images (bypassing Vercel)
Trigger background removal processing on uploaded image

answer.question: How FormData is built

details.content-type-header: NOT SET (browser auto-sets multipart/form-data with boundary)
details.note: Browser automatically sets Content-Type: multipart/form-data; boundary=... when using FormData

answer.question: Client-side logging before fetch
answer.response: NO LOGGING PRESENT before Step 1 fetch to /api/images/direct-upload
answer.blob-creation-logging: Lines 364-373 create blob but do not log size or type

answer.question: Complete implementation of /api/images/direct-upload
answer.description: The Vercel endpoint is a thin proxy that forwards requests to a Cloudflare Worker. It does NOT handle uploads directly, but relays to REMBG_ORIGIN.

critical-issue.description: Server expects req.body to be a JSON object, then does JSON.stringify(req.body). If client sends empty POST (no body), req.body will be undefined or {}. Line 39 sends: body: JSON.stringify(req.body) This results in sending "{}" or "undefined" to the worker.

answer.question: Code section that calls Cloudflare
Environment variables expected
answer.error-handling: Returns 500 with generic "Proxy error" message on fetch failure. Does NOT validate presence of environment variables (uses defaults). Does NOT return 400 for missing body - forwards empty request.

answer.question: Which Cloudflare product is used
answer.product: Cloudflare Images (Direct Creator Upload + Images Binding)

answer.question: Server code that creates signed direct upload URL
answer.critical-issue: Worker sends empty FormData() in body. Cloudflare API may accept this, but the Vercel proxy sends JSON.stringify({}) instead. This mismatch could cause the 500 error.

answer.question: Current vercel.json content
answer.warning: Legacy "builds" array is deprecated and may override project settings. Vercel recommends using "buildCommand" and "outputDirectory" instead.
answer.issue: The routing sends /api/** to app.js, but api/images/direct-upload.js requires server/app.js. This creates a module resolution chain that may cause issues.

answer.question: package.json scripts and TypeScript version
answer.code: { "name": "openpaint", "version": "1.0.0", "main": "app.js", "engines": { "node": "22.x" }, "scripts": { "start": "node app.js", "dev": "node app.js", "build": "echo 'Static assets ready for deployment'", "build:css": "npx --yes @tailwindcss/cli -i \"./css/tailwind.css\" -o \"./css/tailwind.build.css\" --minify", "vercel-build": "npm run build:css", "deploy": "vercel --prod", "test": "npm run test:unit && npm run test:integration", "lint": "eslint .", "lint:fix": "eslint . --fix" }, "devDependencies": { "@testing-library/dom": "^10.4.1", "@types/jest": "^30.0.0", "autoprefixer": "^10.4.21", "cypress": "^14.5.4", "eslint": "^8.57.1", "jest": "^29.7.0", "tailwindcss": "^4.1.16" } }

subpackage-tsconfig.code: { "compilerOptions": { "target": "es2021", "lib": ["es2021"], "jsx": "react-jsx", "module": "es2022", "moduleResolution": "Bundler", "resolveJsonModule": true, "allowJs": true, "checkJs": false, "noEmit": true, "isolatedModules": true, "allowSyntheticDefaultImports": true, "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true, "strict": true, "skipLibCheck": true, "types": [ "./worker-configuration.d.ts" ] }, "exclude": ["test"], "include": ["worker-configuration.d.ts", "src/**/*.ts"] }

esm-files.note: No .mjs or .cjs files found in root or api/ directories

answer.question: Where Tailwind CSS output is referenced
File src/canvas/viewport/useCanvasViewport.ts content
answer.file-extension: .ts (should be .tsx for React code, but no JSX present)

critical-issue.description: File imports from 'react' (line 12) and uses React namespace (line 39-40). However, React and @types/react are NOT installed in the main project. This will cause "Cannot find namespace 'React'" errors during TypeScript compilation.
critical-issue.why-not-tsx: File uses TypeScript types from React but does not contain JSX elements. The .ts extension is technically correct, but requires @types/react to be installed.

root-cause.description: The file src/canvas/viewport/useCanvasViewport.ts imports React hooks and uses React types, but React is not a dependency of the project. This appears to be orphaned/experimental code that is not part of the main application (which uses vanilla JavaScript).
root-cause.recommendation: Either: 1. Remove src/canvas/ directory if not in use 2. Add React dependencies if this code should be used 3. Move to a separate React-based sub-project with proper dependencies

answer.question: Vercel function logs for failing invocation
answer.note: Function logs are only accessible via Vercel dashboard or Vercel CLI. User must retrieve logs from: 1. Vercel Dashboard > Project > Deployments > [Deployment] > Functions > Logs 2. Command: vercel logs [deployment-url]
answer.request-to-user: Please provide the full stack trace and error message from Vercel function logs. Include any console.error output from the failing function invocation.

answer.question: Function runtime and region configuration
answer.runtime: Node.js (inferred from vercel.json builds array using @vercel/node)
answer.region: Not explicitly configured (Vercel auto-selects based on request origin)
answer.region-from-logs:
sin1 (Singapore) - from error logs provided
10 seconds 60 seconds Unknown (depends on Vercel plan)
1024 MB Yes (in vercel.json with "memory" key)
50mb (from server/app.js line 10) 5MB default (can be increased)
Original error logs show content-length: 0, indicating NO image data sent This is not a size/timeout issue but a missing payload issue

local-reproduction.status: N/A - Issue occurs on Vercel deployment only
local-reproduction.reason: Depends on REMBG_ORIGIN environment variable pointing to Cloudflare Worker

vercel-reproduction.step: Observe network tab: POST to /api/images/direct-upload fails with 500

sample-image.note: Error occurs before image upload, so size irrelevant

answer.question: Minimal curl request that reproduces failure
answer.test-worker-directly:
# Test Cloudflare Worker directly (bypassing Vercel proxy): curl -X POST \ https://sofapaint-api.leigh-atkins.workers.dev/images/direct-upload \ -H "x-api-key: dev-secret" \ -H "Content-Type: application/json"
# If this works, problem is in Vercel proxy. # If this fails, problem is in Worker configuration.

primary-cause.title: Client Sends Empty POST, Proxy Forwards Empty JSON

step.action: POSTs to /api/images/direct-upload with NO body (only x-api-key header)
Reads req.body (undefined or {}), stringifies it to "{}"
step.result: Sends empty JSON object to worker

step.action: Receives empty JSON, creates FormData(), POSTs to Cloudflare Images API
step.result: Cloudflare Images API may reject empty request or worker crashes
Unknown (may succeed with empty metadata, may fail)

step.action: Crashes or returns 500 due to malformed proxy request

primary-cause.validation: Original error logs confirm content-length: 0 in request to /api/images/direct-upload. This is NOT a "failed to send blob" issue - the blob upload happens in Step 2 (direct to uploadURL). This is a "failed to get uploadURL" issue in Step 1.

cause.title: No Error Handling for Empty Body
cause.description: Server proxy does not validate req.body before forwarding. Should check if body exists and return 400 Bad Request if missing.

cause.title: Cloudflare Worker May Expect Empty Body
cause.description: Worker code (sofapaint-api/src/index.ts line 53) sends "new FormData()" (empty). This suggests the endpoint SHOULD accept empty requests. Vercel proxy sending JSON.stringify({}) instead may cause incompatibility.

fix.title: Fix Vercel Proxy Body Handling
fix.rationale: Cloudflare Worker endpoint expects empty POST (no body). Current code sends JSON.stringify(undefined) or "{}". Remove body parameter entirely to match worker expectations.

fix.title: Add Logging to Debug Proxy Failures
Upgrade vercel.json to Modern Format
fix.note: Legacy "builds" array is deprecated. Modern config uses rewrites and functions. However, current setup works, so this is lower priority.

fix.title: Remove Unused React/TypeScript Code
fix.action: Delete src/canvas/ directory if not in use by main application. Main app uses vanilla JavaScript, not React.
fix.alternative: If code is intended for future use, create separate package: 1. Move to new directory (e.g., packages/react-canvas/) 2. Create package.json with React dependencies 3. Add tsconfig.json for React/TypeScript

step.title: Apply FIX-001 and FIX-002
step.action: Edit server/app.js to remove body from proxy fetch and add logging

step.title: Deploy to Vercel
step.action: git commit, git push, wait for deployment

step.title: Test Remove Background
step.action: Click "Remove BG" button, check browser network tab and Vercel function logs
step.expected-result: - POST to /api/images/direct-upload returns 200 with uploadURL - POST to uploadURL succeeds with image upload - Background removal completes successfully

step.title: Review Vercel Logs
step.action: Check for "[Proxy]" log messages to confirm proper flow

step.title: Test Direct Worker Access
step.purpose: Verify worker works independently of Vercel proxy

critical-findings.finding: Vercel proxy forwards empty JSON "{}" to Cloudflare Worker
Worker expects empty POST (no body), but proxy adds JSON body

build-issues.issue: No TypeScript in main project but @types/jest installed

summary.immediate-action: Apply FIX-001: Remove body from proxy fetch in server/app.js. This aligns Vercel proxy behavior with Cloudflare Worker expectations.
